objective: Implement the GAP-011 skill-governance slice by adding deterministic validation for repo-owned skill density, trigger overlap, executable assets, validation proof, and prompt-prose-only risk without broadening the public harness surface.
ecosystemReviewFindings: Tool exposure and skill availability claims must be receipt-bound instead of inferred from agent memory.
Instruction provenance and skill usage must be visible enough for future agents to know which workflow surface owns an action.
deepModuleBoundary: Repo-owned skill validation only. The implementation should live behind scripts/validate-packaged-skill.cjs or a small helper it owns, and should inspect .agents/skills/**/SKILL.md plus adjacent executable/proof assets. It must not create a new runtime packet, public CLI rail, or generalized skill marketplace.
implementationThesis: GAP-011 is a low-severity but real control-plane gap: individual skill files can validate while the repo has no deterministic check for prompt-only skills, weak workflow ownership, or overlapping trigger language. The smallest useful fix is to make the existing pnpm skill:validate lane produce fail-closed checks for repo-owned skills using required structured frontmatter, deterministic trigger-surface overlap, and validation-command-to-asset linkage.

executableRequirements: validation_command must appear in the SKILL.md body or a referenced validation section
must include at least one linked executable proof asset: scripts/, references/evals.yaml, references/agent-install.json, or an explicit validator reference
advisoryRequirements: must include a Workflow section and at least one reference file under references/
cannot bypass prompt-prose-only detection by using advisory wording in free text without frontmatter

normalization: no stemming, embeddings, or LLM scoring
overlapPolicy.allowlist: A pair may pass only when an explicit deterministic allowlist entry names both skill names, the shared tokens, and the reason the overlap is intentional.

currentEvidence.observed: pnpm check already runs pnpm skill:validate.
currentEvidence.gap: The skill validator checks the packaged coding-harness skill contract, but does not score density, overlap, executable assets, workflow ownership, or prompt-prose-only risk across repo-owned skills.

currentEvidence.observed: The existing validator enforces required packaged-skill files, stale command guidance, install JSON, eval coverage, and reference contracts.
currentEvidence.gap: The validator has no repo-owned skill inventory, overlap report, advisory/executable distinction, or validation-path rule.

currentEvidence.observed: The packaged skill has executable assets, evals, setup references, and validation commands.
currentEvidence.gap: Those assets are not compared against other repo-owned skills for trigger overlap or workflow ownership.

currentEvidence.observed: The architecture skill is intentionally advisory and has references plus a review workflow, but no script/eval asset.
currentEvidence.gap: The validator should distinguish advisory skills from production executable skills instead of forcing every skill to look like the packaged install skill.

inScope: src/dev/*validate-packaged-skill*test.ts or an equivalent focused script test if needed
.agents/skills/**/SKILL.md only if metadata is needed for deterministic classification
.agents/skills/**/references/** only if needed to document validation or workflow ownership
outOfScope: creating a new public harness command for skill governance
rewriting skill content for style only
absorbing unrelated Project Brain WIP or .harness media/research files into this slice
claiming PR, CI, Linear, review-thread, merge-readiness, or final goal completion truth
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm skill:validate fails when any repo-owned skill lacks machine-readable skill_kind, owned_workflow, or validation_command frontmatter.
pnpm skill:validate fails when a repo-owned executable skill has no linked executable or fixture/proof asset and no referenced runnable validation path.
pnpm skill:validate fails overlapping trigger language when normalized trigger-surface overlap meets or exceeds the blocking threshold and no explicit allowlist entry exists.
Advisory skills can pass without scripts or evals only when they declare skill_kind: advisory, owned_workflow, validation_command, a Workflow section, and reference material.
Prompt-prose-only skills are flagged with skill_prompt_only_risk when they lack scripts, evals, structured references, validation commands, and valid classification.
The validator output names the skill, canonical finding code, severity, and remediation surface without relying on LLM judgment.

Focused tests include negative fixtures for missing classification, prose-only advisory claims, blocking overlap, low-signal nonblocking overlap, and inert executable assets without validation-command linkage.
Existing repo-owned skills pass after intentional metadata or validation-path adjustments.
The slice receipt records Project Brain inputs, GAP-011, validation commands, skill-lens evidence, independent reviewer evidence, and any remaining PR/remote blockers.

automationPlan.name: intent artifact JSON validation
proves: slice intent is syntactically valid before review

automationPlan.name: skill density validation
proves: packaged skill validation and GAP-011 skill-density/overlap checks are enforced in the existing repo gate

automationPlan.name: focused validator regression
proves: negative fixtures catch prompt-only and overlap failures without needing live repo mutation

automationPlan.name: goal board validation
proves: audit freshness, active-artifacts route, and receipt shape remain valid

automationPlan.name: slice assurance
proves: post-R064 skill-lens and independent reviewer evidence is complete before a done claim

reviewQuestions: Does this intent stay narrow enough for GAP-011 without inventing a broad skill governance platform?
Are advisory skills treated fairly while still catching prompt-prose-only risk?
Are the proposed deterministic overlap and asset checks strong enough to prevent recurrence of the audit gap?

reviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Advisory classification was prose-only and overlap semantics lacked deterministic threshold policy.
reviewFindingsAddressed.resolution: Added skillMetadataContract, overlapPolicy, canonical findingCodes, and fail-closed acceptance criteria.

reviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Overlap acceptance permitted report-only pass states.
reviewFindingsAddressed.resolution: Changed overlap acceptance to fail when normalized trigger-surface overlap meets the blocking threshold without an allowlist.

reviewFindingsAddressed.finding: Machine-readable advisory/executable classification and prompt-only rule order were missing.
reviewFindingsAddressed.resolution: Required skill_kind, owned_workflow, validation_command, class-specific requirements, and canonical finding codes.

trackedReviewArtifactRepair.reason: The first review targeted root artifacts/, which is ignored. PU-027 now requires reviewers to write curated artifacts under tracked .harness/review/ before implementation can start.

blockerClearance.verdict: The reviewers that previously returned do-not-proceed now report proceed after stale ignored-path references were removed from active intent metadata and tracked artifact verification passed.

ignoredRootArtifactAttempt.reason: The prior reviewer mailbox summaries reported root-level review paths that are intentionally ignored by repository policy; those summaries remain historical routing evidence only.

trackedArtifactRereview.reportedOutcome: do not proceed before stale ignored-path metadata and pending repair state are cleaned
do not proceed before all required tracked artifacts exist and the failed verification state is cleared
trackedArtifactRereview.residualNote: policy-path mismatch risk for agents/templates and agents/contracts.json is non-blocking for intent

trackedArtifactRereview.reportedOutcome: implementation can proceed after tracked artifacts are present
trackedArtifactRereview.residualNote: allowlist storage location is a low non-blocking clarity gap

artifactVerification.result: All three tracked reviewer artifacts exist and are non-empty.

rereviewOutcome.coordinatorDecision: Treat the tracked artifacts as the active review evidence and request a focused blocker-clearance pass from reviewers that previously returned do-not-proceed before implementation starts.

postReviewMutationPolicy: any material mutation to objective, inScope, outOfScope, acceptanceCriteria, automationPlan, reviewPlan, stopConditions, or rollback resets reviewStatus to pending
linearMutationPlan: No Linear write during this local implementation slice. JSC-363 remains linked; full tracker alignment still requires a later live refresh and Phase 1 wording reconciliation.
stopConditions: Stop if the validator would require a new package dependency for simple frontmatter or lexical scans.
Stop if deterministic overlap rules produce false positives that cannot be explained by exact trigger evidence and a narrow allowlist entry.
Stop if enforcing executable assets on advisory skills would force misleading scripts or fixtures.
Stop if implementation would touch unrelated Project Brain WIP, global skill repositories, or broad public command surfaces.
Stop if reviewer artifacts are missing after one focused retry.
rollback: Revert validator and test changes if deterministic rules block legitimate existing skills without a narrow metadata escape.
Keep any metadata additions that clarify advisory versus executable ownership only if the validator still depends on them.
Do not roll back unrelated dirty worktree changes.
assumptions: Repo-owned skills are the skills under .agents/skills/ in this repository.
The packaged coding-harness skill is executable and should keep strong script/eval/reference requirements.
The improve-codebase-architecture skill is advisory/review-oriented and should prove density through workflow ownership and references rather than executable scripts.
pnpm skill:validate is the correct existing lane to host this GAP-011 guard because pnpm check already executes it.
